calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Mar-26-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO FEDERAL CREDIT UNION'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION

Order on claim of exemption:

The third-party claimants have not filed further factual support for their claims of ownership of levied accounts. The claims of exemption and third-party claims are denied.

Order to show cause:

The court discharges the order to show cause, finding excusable neglect.

Court will perpare order.

Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

***Judgment Debtor Winnie Wenran Tsai Wong appeared in court, but she did properly contest the Court's ruling. The matter was not called. ****

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Mar-06-2025 Continued To Mar-26-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Winnie Wong, 415-608-0127 Pro per Defendnat/Judgment Debtor
No appearance by Plaintiff/Judgment Creditor

Per the Court's tentative ruling a hearing was required. Counsel for the plaintiff was not present at the time the matter was called at 9:30 a.m. The courtroom clerk reached out to plaintiff's counsel Bret Allen Yaple by telephone. The receptionist was unsuccessful in reaching the assigned appearance attorney who was assigned to appear on behalf of plaintiff's counsel. Court is informed the appearing attorney for Plaintiff's counsel would not be able to make an appearance. A Cantonese interpreter was not available to assist Defendant. Defendant's daughter advises to the Court that she has been the party that has been dealing with Plaintiff San Francisco Federal Credit Union and is the person most knowledgeable in regards to this matter. Court allows Defendant's daughter to appear on behalf of Defendant Winnie Wong.

Discussed not Argued.

PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO FEDERAL CREDIT UNION HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION is continued to March 26, 2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 301. TK Credit Recovery is ordered to show cause why they should not be sanctioned in the amount of $250.00, payable to the court for violation of a court order without good cause or substantial justification.

Court prepares and signs order.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-16-2020 Off Calendar

Notice Of Prove Up Hearing
Off Calendar Jun-30-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Cordwee Wong and Winnie Wenran Tsai Wong.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

David Marcolini, Esq. appeared for plaintiff San Francisco Federal Credit Union.
William Cooper, Collections Manager for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
Both Messrs. Marcolini and Cooper appeared for this morning's proceedings via CourtCall.
No appearance by defendants Cordwee Wong or Winnie Wenran Tsai Wong.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach/Common Counts was filed on August 19, 2019, and personally served on defendant Winnie Wenran Tsai Wong on September 10, 2019.
Substitute service of plaintiff's Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents on defendant Cordwee Wong occurred on September 10, 2019 and was effectuated on September 20, 2019.
Default as to defendants Cordwee Wong and Winnie Wenran Tsai Wong was entered on December 23, 2019.
DOE defendants were dismissed on February 10, 2020.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff San Francisco Federal Credit Union, and against the defaulted defendants Cordwee Wong and Winnie Wenran Tsai Wong, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $198,020.26. (Amount Pled in Complaint).
Prejudgment Interest - $29,560.05.
Attorney Fees - $4,580.00.
Costs - $730.00.

Total - $232,890.31.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
